summary: Soundboard recording (with a reversed stereo image) delivers a solid early‑90s Dead show highlighted by soaring vocals, a stellar Space jam and the rare performance of The Weight.
review: The mix is surprisingly clear for an audience tape – despite the left/right channels being swapped it captures the rich orchestral feel of the Hornsby/Vince era with crisp piano chords and tight vocal harmonies.  Fans rave about the extended Space improvisation, the unexpected rendition of The Weight and strong vocal blend, while a few note that the show isn’t a “barn‑burner” and some were disappointed by the crowd atmosphere.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance and vocal harmonies, and several call the sound “amazing” or a “great mix”; technical quirks (reversed stereo) are noted but don’t detract significantly, yielding an overall strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp and well‑balanced mix; left/right channels appear reversed compared to typical stage layout; no major dropouts or muffling reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Early‑90s set with strong vocal harmonies, a notable Space jam in the second set and a rare placement of The Weight; overall flow is solid though not an all‑out high‑energy barn‑burner.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Picking/guitar work described as good; tone noted positively.
band_member_comments.Keys: Bruce Hornsby and Vince Welnick both featured; praised for big piano chords, organ/accordion interplay, and vocal blending.
